title: 5. Lab - Deploying a Secure Customer Service Orchestrator

blocks.content: In this final lab, you will deploy a Customer Service agent pipeline inside a sandboxed docker container. The agent connects to a Postgres Database via MCP stdio, uses secure OAuth2 helper tokens to verify orders, and outputs tracking updates to the visual panel using A2UI dashboards.
Compile the production agent and tool structures into a container image:
Step 2: Run Postgres Database MCP Server
Ensure the backend database is running and verify it is discoverable by local networks:
Step 3: Run the sandboxed Agent Container
Start the container running in user namespace mode, mapping config files and network ports:
Send an execution payload to verify route tables, data queries, and secure compliance reporting:

blocks.code:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8090/execute \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"prompt": "Check if user order 9023 exists in database and generate tracking dashboard card."}'

blocks.title: Deployment Verification
blocks.content: Verify container execution logs with 'docker logs service-agent-run' to ensure no authentication exceptions or parameter validation errors occurred during startup.
